title: Monetdb crash when using field fcuntion
description: Dear MonetDB Developers, I came across an crash in MonetDB. Whenever convenient, it would mean a lot if you could take a look. I sincerely appreciate your efforts in continuously improving MonetDB’s robustness. poc ```sql CREATE TABLE test_fields (id INT, name VARCHAR(50)); INSERT INTO test_fields VALUES (1, 'foo'), (2, 'bar'), (3, 'baz'), (4, 'qux'); SELECT field('bar', (SELECT name FROM test_fields WHERE id = 2)) AS index_in_subquery; -- Expected Result: -- 0 ``` mclient generate the following ouput: unexpected end of file and mserver is terminated fish: Job 1, 'AFL_IGNORE_PROBLEMS=1 /usr/loca…' terminated by signal SIGABRT ## merovingian log output : #2025-09-18 07:24:47: client0110: exp_bin: CRITICAL: Could not find %4.%4 #2025-09-18 07:24:47: client0110: exp_bin: ERROR: Query: 'select field('bar', (select name from test_fields where id = 2)) as index_in_subquery;' mserver5: /home/MonetDB/sql/backends/monet5/rel_bin.c:1850: stmt *exp_bin(backend *, sql_exp *, stmt *, stmt *, stmt *, stmt *, stmt *, stmt *, int, int, int): Assertion `s' failed. ## Relevant source code
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value) VALUES (1, 'alpha'), (2, 'beta'), (3, 'gamma'), (4, NULL);
SELECT INSTR((SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE id = 2), 'beta') AS index_in_subquery;
SELECT INSTR((SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE value IS NULL), NULL) AS index_in_null_subquery;
SELECT INSTR((SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE created_at < CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), 'alpha') AS index_in_time_subquery;
key_bug_features: SELECT field function with subquery causing crash
